Pricol demerger

Pricol Limited is splitting into two separate listed companies:

Pricol Autotech Limited (new company) — will house the DICVS business: instrument clusters, infotainment systems, e-cockpits, telematics, battery management systems, and connected vehicle tech.

Pricol Limited (existing company) — will retain the ACFMS (pumps, brakes, fluid management) and Precision Products (P3L) businesses,

Why are they doing this?

The core argument is that these two businesses have fundamentally different DNA:

DICVS is a technology/software play — fast-changing, innovation-led, rides EV/connectivity megatrends, needs R&D and semiconductor partnerships.

ACFMS + P3L is a manufacturing/scale play — rides production volumes, regulatory mandates (disc brakes, ABS), and export opportunities

**Neogen Chemicals**

Neogen Chemicals targets Rs.2,400–2,900 crores revenue from battery chemicals by FY29

Neogen strategically entered battery chemicals around 2017, driven by rising lithium prices and the global shift towards EV batteries

Electrolyte was chosen as a core focus because it is central to the battery, recipe-driven, has shelf-life constraints, and is difficult to import at scale

The battery chemicals business aims for a 20% pre-tax Return on Capital (ROC), utilizing a formula-based pricing model that tracks lithium price movements

The revenue plan is heavily dependent on the battery cell manufacturing ecosystem scaling up significantly in India, with at least 60 GWh expected in 1.5-2 years and 150 GWh by FY29/FY30

Neogen's formula-based pricing for electrolyte is projected to be more competitive than China's spot prices over a five-year horizon, offering customers transparency and sourcing flexibility

Electrolyte import at gigawatt scale is deemed impractical due to logistics, temperature stability, and shelf-life constraints, emphasizing the need for local manufacturing

The primary risk identified is a potential delay in India's overall battery manufacturing ramp-up, rather than technology failure, though management expresses optimism

Source:- Phillip Capital

**Shree Refrigeration**

**Robust FY26 Order Book: The company holds an order book of Rs 270 crore as of March 31, 2026, which is 1.8x its current order book, with confidence in ahead-of-schedule delivery
**
Defence Sector Specialization: Shree Refrigeration is recognized as a defence-focused manufacturing company with all three necessary naval registrations, allowing them to supply AC plants, HVAC systems, and electrical control panels

Unlike many competitors who act as system integrators, Shree Refrigeration emphasizes full in-house manufacturing, including critical components and test equipment

EBITDA margin decreased from 27% to 21% primarily due to a sharp drop in spare and service revenue (from 30% to 7%), but the company is targeting 15% for this segment going forward

Management reiterated its expectation for 40% CAGR growth, explicitly stating that this guidance does not include any potential revenue from the data centre business

The company maintains a blended market share of approximately 64% in the naval HVAC segment, with its retrofit business having a slightly stronger share. 

The total addressable market for HVAC in marine and naval applications is estimated at around Rs 5,000 crore today, with potential for further growth

Source:- Phillip Capital

ADITYA ULTRA STEEL | Approves Merger with VMS TMT

• Board approved a Scheme of Amalgamation with VMS TMT Limited, subject to regulatory and other required approvals.

• The merger aims to consolidate operations under the 'Kamdhenu' brand across Gujarat and strengthen market presence.

• The combined entity will leverage a distribution network of 3 distributors and 300 dealers.

• The merger is expected to generate operational synergies through improved procurement, production, logistics and optimization of solar power assets.

• The combined manufacturing capacity will be 308,000 TPA, comprising 108,000 TPA of Aditya Ultra Steel and 200,000 TPA of VMS TMT.

• Under the approved share exchange ratio, shareholders will receive 75 equity shares of VMS TMT for every 100 equity shares of Aditya Ultra Steel.

SPICE ISLANDS INDUSTRIES | Signs Hotel Lease Agreement in Dwarka

• Signed a Hotel Lease & Operating Agreement for 'The Ocean Prime' hotel in Dwarka, Gujarat.

• The lease will commence on July 1, 2026, for a tenure of 11 months and 29 days, ending June 30, 2027.

• The property comprises 26 rooms along with restaurant facilities.

• The monthly lease rent is ₹3 lakh, with an annual escalation of 10%.

• The company has paid a refundable security deposit of ₹50 lakh.

• The agreement is with Ladhu Kara Charities (Seth Ladhukara Charity Trust), an unrelated party, and is aimed at expanding the company's hospitality business.

BIG UPDATE FOR HDFC BANK 

SENTIMENTAL POSITIVE 

*HDFC Bank update: No evidence found by external law firm*

- HDFC Bank announced the completion of an independent legal review conducted by external law firms following the concerns raised by former Independent Director Atanu Chakraborty in his resignation letter. After a three-month review involving thousands of documents, Board and committee minutes, and interviews with independent directors and senior management, the law firms concluded that Mr. Chakraborty’s allegations were not substantiated. 

- *Key findings:* No evidence was found in Board or committee records supporting his concerns. Mr. Chakraborty had opportunities to record any dissent or ethical concerns in meeting minutes but did not do so. 
- Interviews with directors and senior management did not corroborate his statements. 
- No contemporaneous evidence showed that he had raised objections regarding the Dubai matter or any other Board decisions.
- Despite repeated requests, Mr. Chakraborty did not participate in the legal review. 
- The external law firms found no factual basis to support Mr. Chakraborty’s statements, and the contemporaneous evidence was inconsistent with the allegations made in his resignation and subsequent public comments. 

- *View:* The conclusion of the independent legal review removes a key governance overhang for HDFC Bank. The review found no evidence supporting the allegations raised by the former independent director, which should help restore investor confidence and reduce governance-related concerns 

Brokerage Note

Indian oil marketing companies are unlikely to reduce petrol and diesel prices immediately, despite the recent decline in crude oil prices.

The three state-run OMCs reportedly incurred around ₹1 lakh crore of cumulative under-recoveries on petrol, diesel and LPG during March–May 2026, primarily due to elevated crude prices and limited retail price increases.
Companies are expected to use the current period of relatively lower crude prices to recover these losses before considering any retail price cuts.

Analysts also caution that global oil inventories remain tight, leaving room for crude prices to rise again if geopolitical risks or supply disruptions re-emerge.

The IRGC rejected a newly announced shipping corridor through the Strait of Hormuz, saying it was created without Tehran’s coordination.

It said only routes designated by Iran are authorized for transit.

Ships were instructed to maintain contact with the IRGC Navy on maritime Channel 16.

The IRGC warned that vessels using unauthorized routes could face enforcement measures, raising the risk of interceptions or other actions.

This will reduce college enrollments, especially at non-elite universities.

Many of these institutions may face financial challenges or closures.

Local economies that depend on these universities could also suffer significant losses.

Indian oil marketing companies are unlikely to reduce petrol and diesel prices immediately, despite the recent decline in crude oil prices.

The three state-run OMCs reportedly incurred around ₹1 lakh crore of cumulative under-recoveries on petrol, diesel and LPG during March–May 2026, primarily due to elevated crude prices and limited retail price increases.
Companies are expected to use the current period of relatively lower crude prices to recover these losses before considering any retail price cuts.

Analysts also caution that global oil inventories remain tight, leaving room for crude prices to rise again if geopolitical risks or supply disruptions re-emerge.

India has learned one lesson repeatedly: strategic dependence comes at a cost.

- 90% of our oil demand is met through imports → We must diversify energy sources.
- AI models are largely built overseas and are weaponized or restricted (US restricted Mythos new models to outside countries or companies) → We need sovereign Indian AI models - India rolls out three sovereign AI models, Sarvam AI, Gnani AI, BharatGen, to take on Big Tech
* Semiconductors and rare earths have been external dependencies → India is now investing in domestic chip manufacturing and strengthening its rare earth ecosystem (China had weaponized rare earth exports)

Energy security. Technology security. Supply chain security.

The next decade won't just be about economic growth—it will be about strategic self-reliance!

*Vedanta Iron and Steel Ltd. & Vedanta Ltd. analyst meet 2026*

*Vedanta Iron and Steel Limited (VISL)* 
• VISL currently operates with 15mtpa iron ore capacity and 3mtpa steel capacity, supported by an integrated mining to steel value chain.

• Iron ore capacity is targeted to expand from ~15mtpa to 18.5mtpa by FY29, supported by beneficiation expansion and pellet capacity addition. Liberia assets is expected to contribute 10mtpa pellet feed concentrate by FY32.

• Integrated steel production planned to increase from ~3mtpa to 5mtpa by FY29, with a longer term expansion potential of upto 15mtpa.

• ESL plant Phase I expansion is nearing completion and is expected to double capacity from 1.6mtpa to 3.2mtpa by FY27, supported by existing infrastructure and land availability. 

• The company currently operates a 2mt beneficiation plant in Goa and plans to add 4mt of beneficiation capacity in Karnataka to improve ore quality and economics. Additionally, a new 2mt pellet plant is being set up in Odisha to strengthen raw material integration for Bokaro steel operations.

• Goa iron ore reserves carry ~57% Fe grade, with current production at 57–58% Fe, while Karnataka reserves are lower grade at 42-43% Fe, upgraded to ~51% Fe sales grade through beneficiation.

• Planned 3mt operations in Liberia are expected to generate ~USD 100mn in EBITDA by FY30, this would also help replace Karnataka mines’ EBITDA contribution of ~USD 80mn after lease expiry in 2032.

• Subject to receiving Stage 1 forest clearance, the key blast furnace (BF) expansion is largely ready and is expected to be commissioned within 3 months.

• Management targets ~4x EBITDA growth by FY29, driven by steel capacity expansion, and a higher contribution from value added products (VAP). The share of VAP is expected to increase from 59% in FY26 to ~100% by FY29E, supporting improved realizations and margin expansion. VAP mix would largely include wire rods, TMT, and high grade alloys

• The total planned capex is USD 1.2bn through FY29, of which USD 422mn has been approved and USD 268mn has already been deployed. Around 70% of the capex is expected to be funded through internal accruals, while the remaining 30% will be supported by a proforma net debt of ~Rs 25bn (as of May 2026), after demerger related adjustments. 

• planned capex is estimated at USD 130-150mn for FY27 which would  peak at ~USD 350mn each in FY28 and FY29.

• Capacity additions in DI pipes, wire rods, and TMT products are expected to strengthen and deepen the VISL’s value added steel portfolio. 

• Pig iron margins are expected to increase from USD 30-40/t to ~USD 60-65/t over time.

*Vedanta Ltd - Ferro Chrome*

• Vedanta plans to expand ferro chrome capacity from 145kt to 500kt by FY30, with the new expansion project expected to be commissioned in late FY27. The company targets EBITDA of ~USD 180mn by FY30 and aims to become one of India's largest ferro chrome producers.

• The business has strong raw material security through ownership of five mines (four chromite and one manganese) with reserves/resources of ~52 mt, providing visibility for over 25 years. Underground mining at Ostapal has commenced and is expected to improve ore quality and support future growth.

• Management expects realizations to increase from USD 1,100/t currently to USD 1,250/t by FY30 through higher-grade ore, improved product mix and a greater share of premium-sized products. Expansion into European and far east markets is also expected to support pricing improvement.

• Domestic market share is targeted to rise from 16% to 37%, supported by India's stainless steel demand growth of ~10% CAGR. The company is transitioning from trader-led sales to direct OEM relationships and long-term offtake agreements with major stainless steel producers.
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
• Production costs are expected to decline from USD 1,100/t in FY26 to USD 850/t in FY27 through operational efficiencies and mine optimization initiatives. Additionally, the planned 150 MW renewable power capacity will enable ~50% green ferrochrome production, enhancing access to CBAM-compliant export markets.

*Zinc International*

• Zinc International owns one of the world's largest undeveloped zinc resource portfolios, anchored by the Gamsberg asset in South Africa. The resource base provides a long mine life and supports management's long-term ambition of scaling production towards 1mt  of metal-in-concentrate (MIC).

• Growth will be driven through Gamsberg phase-II expansion, development of the Swartberg open-pit and underground mines, and the Namibia-based Gergarub project. These projects are expected to significantly increase production volumes and strengthen the asset portfolio over the next five years.

• MIC production is targeted to increase from ~340 kt in FY27 to ~500-550 kt by FY29 and ~800 kt by FY30. Underground mining initiatives, higher-grade ore access and debottlenecking projects are expected to improve both throughput and operational efficiency.

• Cost of production is expected to decline from USD 2,000/t currently to USD 1,200/t over the long term through economies of scale, improved mine planning and higher utilization levels. As a result, EBITDA is projected to increase from USD 160 mn in FY26 to nearly USD 1 bn by FY30. FY27 costs are targeted at USD 1,500/t

• Vedanta is evaluating a zinc smelter in South Africa, supported by its large resource base and potential preferential power arrangements.

*Copper*

• Management expects India's copper demand to reach ~3.2 mt by 2030, supported by growth in power, renewable energy, EVs and infrastructure. Demand in the GCC region, particularly Saudi Arabia, is also expected to grow strongly, creating significant opportunities for copper processing and manufacturing. 

• Vedanta aims to build a 1mt copper platform comprising 500 kt capacity in India and 500 kt in the middle east. The strategy encompasses refining, rod manufacturing, mining exploration and potential smelting projects to create an integrated copper value chain across both regions.

• The company is progressing with key growth projects, including the Saudi copper rod mill, which is expected to be commissioned by Oct-26 to cater to the region's expanding downstream copper ecosystem. Vedanta has also secured exploration licences in Saudi Arabia and is evaluating mining opportunities to strengthen raw material security and integration.

• Silvassa refinery production has increased to 222 kt and is targeted to reach ~230 kt in the coming months, utilizing the existing 250 kt capacity more efficiently. In parallel, management continues discussions with authorities regarding the potential restart of the Sterlite copper plant at Tuticorin under a proposed green copper framework.

• Copper volumes are expected to increase from 364 kt in FY27 to 713 kt by FY30, driven by contributions from Silvassa, Fujairah, the Saudi rod mill and future smelter projects. EBITDA is projected to grow from USD 90mn in FY27 to USD 297mn by FY30, supported by higher volumes, improved utilization and expansion-led growth.

Emerald Finance Limited Onboards Ebixcash Mobility Software India Limited, Delhi for Early Wage Access

Emerald Finance Limited has entered into a new employer partnership with Ebixcash Mobility Software India Ltd., Delhi, extending its Early-Wage-Access (EWA) program to their employees.

Through a salary-linked advance model, employees can access a portion of their earned wages at any point during the month - disbursed in coordination with the employer, helping address short-term liquidity needs.

By eliminating dependency on the fixed pay cycle, the program gives employees greater control over their finances. Repayments are managed through automatic payroll deductions at month end - keeping the process disciplined, transparent and stress-free.

This collaboration reflects Emerald Finance's continued commitment to expanding its EWA network across India, making earned wage access a standard employee benefit.

IPO NOTE ON SBI Funds Management Flows (USD mn): India’s largest AMC with a diversified AUM mix

From Film Supplier to Consumer-Centric Solution Provider

GAS (Garware Auto Solutions / Application Studios) - for car PPF and window films. They install directly or through controlled channels.

GHS (Garware Home Solutions) - expanding into home films for furniture, kitchen sinks, marble, and even privacy switchable films.

Why this matters? They capture 25-30% margins instead of lower distributor-led margins. Better brand control, customer data, and ability to upsell premium products.

Moves from just selling film to selling protection + installation + peace of mind for cars and homes.

Garware shift looks more structural (full value chain control) and may deliver steadier margin gains.

Disc: Holding from 2800, The tariff crash gave me a 50% discount and also showed me how much this business can survive pain & what management does in tough situations. 

I've talked about this many times in my past articles, Now the stock price is on the roof, which even I regret - why didn't I buy enough. I just stayed tough with my own portfolio allocation.

TD Power Systems : Management is in line to achieve 2,400 cr of revenue in FY27 and is very much likely that they will increase their guidance in the coming quarters.

At their current guidance, they will grow their revenue by 30%. 

Lets discuss about how their product segments will perform in FY27:

1. Steam Turbine : The market is steady with around 10% - 12% growth. 

2. Gas Turbine and Gas Engine business : Huge growth expected in this segment till FY28 due to heavy order inflows and increasing capacity. 

3. Hydro : This year is expected to be one of the best performing year for the company in hydro. 

4. Motors & Railways : Stable growth.

Regulatory Challenge

- Battery collection targets rising annually
- Non-compliance attracts penalties
- Recycling costs becoming mandatory

Company's Concerns
- Same rules for different chemistries
- Zinc-carbon treated like lithium batteries
- Compliance framework needs differentiation
- Sustainability of dry battery business questioned

Gold-Loan Firms: Well Protected from Price Volatility

Core Findings
- Gold-loan lenders remain well protected from price corrections
- Crisil sees limited risk despite 85% regulatory LTV cap
- Most lenders continue lending at 65–75% LTV
- Conservative underwriting remains the industry norm

Risk Management
- Regular mark-to-market (MTM) of gold collateral
- Adequate LTV buffers maintained
- Efficient auction & recovery process
- Negligible credit costs over the past decade

Stress Test Insights
- Based on 25 years of daily gold prices
- Sharpest 90-day price fall was ~20%
- >10% declines occurred in only ~2% of cases
- RBI's 30-day average price rule adds protection

Key Takeaway
- Crisil believes India's gold-loan industry has built strong structural safeguards through conservative lending, regular collateral monitoring, and prudent recovery practices, making it well-positioned to withstand gold price volatility despite the higher regulatory LTV limit.

- India can evolve from an IT hub to a data-powered AI leader

Key Takeaway

- DAP demand reduced 4.9%
- Urea demand lowered 1.9%
- Overall impact likely limited

Growth Drivers
- NPK volumes grew 22% in FY26
- DAP dependence steadily reducing
- MCFL expands southern presence
- Stronger geographic distribution network

Why It Matters
- NPK mix to increase further
- Backward integration lowers costs
- Sulphuric acid plants improve margins
- Better resilience against demand swings

Key Takeaway
- Paradeep Phosphates' strategic shift toward higher-margin NPK fertilizers, supported by MCFL integration and backward integration, is expected to cushion the impact of lower Kharif fertilizer demand and El Niño-related risks.

Flipkart Minutes: Aggressive Quick Commerce Expansion

Expansion & Growth
- Crossed 1,000 fulfilment centres across 130 cities
- Covers 8,000+ pincodes in under 2 years
- Plans to expand to 1,500 centres soon
- Orders have grown 5x over the past year

Key Growth Drivers
- Tier-2 & Tier-3 markets grew 42x YoY
- Gen Z contributes over 40% of customers
- Demand expanding beyond groceries
- Repeat purchases increased over 20%

Strategic Outlook
- Competes with Blinkit, Instamart & Zepto
- Partners with 500+ D2C brands & 3,000+ farmers
- Over 10% of deliveries via EVs
- Focused on deeper Bharat penetration

Key Takeaway
- Flipkart Minutes is rapidly scaling its quick-commerce network, with strong traction in Tier-2/3 cities and rising demand across multiple categories, positioning itself to challenge market leaders in India's fast-growing quick-commerce space.

KPIL: Exits Kohima-Mariani Transmission

Transaction Details
- Sold remaining ~26% stake
- Buyer: Apraava Energy
- Fully exited KMTL
- Transaction completed June 24

Key Highlights
- Regulatory approvals received
- Completion confirmed June 25
- Entire equity stake divested
- Strategic portfolio realignment

Why It Matters
- Unlocks capital for new projects
- Simplifies investment portfolio
- Reflects asset monetisation strategy
- Supports capital allocation discipline

Key Takeaway
- KPIL has fully exited Kohima-Mariani Transmission Ltd. after selling its remaining ~26% stake to Apraava Energy, completing the strategic divestment following regulatory approvals.

Schneider Electric: Data Centres to Become Largest India Business

Growth Outlook
- Data centres could become Schneider's largest India business by 2030
- India expected to add 6–10 GW of data centre capacity
- Segment currently contributes 15–20% of India revenue
- AI-led electrification to drive long-term growth

Key Growth Drivers
- AI workloads increasing infrastructure demand
- Sovereign AI & low-latency needs boosting local data centres
- Liquid & advanced cooling technologies gaining adoption
- India remains a key manufacturing & R&D hub

Why It Matters
- Benefits electrical infrastructure ecosystem
- Supports long-term power equipment demand
- Reinforces India's strategic role for Schneider globally
- Creates opportunities across AI, data centres & exports

Key Takeaway
- Schneider Electric expects India's AI-driven data centre expansion to make data centres its largest business by 2030, highlighting a multi-year growth opportunity for companies involved in power infrastructure, electrification, cooling solutions and digital infrastructure.

TVS Motor: iQube Crosses 10 Lakh Units

Milestone
- Rolled out 10 lakhth iQube
- Manufactured at Hosur plant
- Launched in 2020
- Among India's leading EV scooters

Network Strength
- 3,300+ sales & service touchpoints
- Presence across 3,000+ cities
- Wide nationwide reach
- Strong customer support

Sustainability Impact
- Users covered 14.94 billion km
- Avoided ~5.23 lakh tonnes of CO₂
- Promotes clean mobility
- Supports India's EV transition

Key Takeaway
- TVS Motor has achieved the 10 lakh iQube production milestone, highlighting the strong adoption of its electric scooter and reinforcing its leadership in India's fast-growing EV market.

**InCred on Non-Ferrous**

**Sector View**
- Negative on aluminium
- Bull case appears overstated
- Valuations look stretched
- 30-40% downside risk flagged

**Key Thesis**
- Focus on primary supply misplaced
- Scrap & secondary aluminium ignored
- Supply tightness easing
- Commodity outlook weakening

**China Outlook**
- Primary deficit narrowing
- Secondary consumption: 13.35 Mt (2025)
- Scrap replacing primary output
- Better overall supply balance

**Global Factors**
- Middle East risk premium fading
- Supply disruptions temporary
- Aluminium prices vulnerable
- Potential correction: ~$800/tonne

**Stocks in Focus**
- NALCO
- Vedanta Aluminium
- Hindalco Industries

**Investment View**
- Rich valuations
- Earnings risk if prices fall
- Commodity cycle turning
- Limited upside at current levels

**Key Takeaway**
- InCred remains **negative on the aluminium sector**, arguing that rising secondary supply in China and fading geopolitical risk could drive an **~$800/tonne correction** in aluminium prices, leaving stocks like **NALCO, Vedanta Aluminium, and Hindalco** exposed to **30-40% downside**.

- Target Price: ₹275/share (Raised)

- Lower-risk growth profile
- High return-accretive projects
- Fastest growth among peers
- Strong execution track record

**Business Transformation**
- Shift toward PPA-led portfolio
- Stable annuity-like cash flows
- Reduced merchant exposure
- Higher earnings visibility

**Capacity Expansion**
- 24 GW under construction
- Majority backed by PPAs
- EBITDA: ~₹3.5/kWh
- Fuel cost pass-through available

- Strong commissioning history
- Healthy leverage position
- Early equipment procurement
- Execution edge over peers

**Cost Advantage**
- Equipment pre-ordered from BHEL & L&T
- Capex 10-30% lower than peers
- Greater bidding flexibility
- Supports future PPA wins

**Growth Pipeline**
- Upcoming 14 GW PPAs
- Uttar Pradesh
- Rajasthan
- Uttarakhand
- West Bengal
- Gujarat

**Key Risks**
- Weak power demand
- Lower merchant realization (FY27-FY28)
- Project commissioning delays
- Capex inflation
- Rising DISCOM receivables

**Key Takeaway**

**CLSA on SBI**

**Rating**
- Outperform
- Target Price: ₹1,275/share

**Loan Growth**
- Robust system-wide growth
- Driven by retail demand
- Corporate lending remains healthy
- Focus on profitable growth

**Management Commentary**
- Tactical approach to corporate lending
- Prioritizing profitability over volume
- Disciplined credit underwriting
- Balanced growth strategy

**Margin Outlook**
- FY27 NIM target: 3%
- Supported by repricing of T-Bill linked loans
- Confident of achieving guidance
- FCNR(B) impact on margins limited

**Liquidity**
- RBI's FCNR(B) measures
- Positive for system liquidity
- Improves funding environment
- Limited direct benefit to SBI margins

**Asset Quality**
- Asset quality remains strong
- Stable despite geopolitical uncertainties
- Credit costs remain under control
- Healthy balance sheet

**Key Risk**
- El Niño remains the primary concern
- Weak monsoon could impact rural demand
- Potential slowdown in rural credit growth
- Agriculture-linked segments to be monitored

**Key Takeaway**
- CLSA maintains an **Outperform** rating with a **₹1,275 target**, citing robust loan growth, confidence in achieving a **3% FY27 NIM**, strong asset quality, and disciplined lending. The key monitorable remains the monsoon, as a weak rainfall season could weigh on rural demand.

**Rating**

- Meaningful execution from FY28
- Better long-term revenue visibility
- Near-term execution remains limited

- Ahead of expectations
- FY29 revenue estimates raised
- FY29 earnings estimates raised
- EPS increased by 1.4%

**Key Concerns**
- Near-term margin risks
- Higher external procurement mix
- Execution challenges remain
- Profitability could be impacted

**Investment View**
- Order inflows improving
- Revenue visibility strengthened
- Execution remains key monitorable
- Cautious on near-term earnings delivery

**Key Takeaway**
- Goldman Sachs maintains a **Sell** rating with a **₹1,275 target**. While the Helina order improves FY29 earnings estimates and order visibility, the brokerage remains concerned about execution timelines and potential margin pressure from a higher share of externally procured components.

reports.text:
INCRED ON HAL

GE414 Engine Price Escalation Is A No Event

GE414 Was Considered For Tejas Medium Mk2

Prototype, Flight Testing Of Mk2 Not Done Yet
India's IT export

Sri Priyanka Geo Commex IPO

The real story is not just revenue growth. It is working capital expansion.

The company’s working capital requirement is projected to rise from ₹17.27 crore in FY25 to ₹36.96 crore in FY27.

That is more than 2x in two years.

Using the FY23–FY25 average working capital turnover ratio of ~14.85x, this level of working capital can support revenue of nearly ₹549 crore in FY27.

For context, FY25 revenue was ₹266 crore.

So, the projected working capital base is implying almost 2x revenue potential.

ADANI GROUP AGM 

Gautam Adani Says - At Adani Power, we are implementing India’s largest ever private sector power capex programme of over Rs 2 lakh crore, with a target of reaching 45 GW of capacity over the next five years.

Adani Energy Solutions transmission order book rose to Rs 72,000 crore.

QUALITY POWER 

BESS+ GIS + HVDC + Insulator= Quality power equipment 

Nuvama visit Quality power equipment

MS on Adani Power
Maintain Overweight; Hike TP to Rs 275 from Rs 173( 59% Target Price Hike )

The Cash Compounding Machine
Portfolio is high growth, lower risk and return accretive
A rare combination that merits closer attention

Incremental business is much lower risk, highly return accretive and fastest growing

Backed by a track record of executing complex projects

CCI Approves Acqn Of Shareholding In Nxtra Data By Alpha Wave Ventures II, LP 

Alpha Wave Secures CCI Approval For Stake In Airtel's Nxtra Data Amid $1 Bn Fundraise

Antique on NALCO
BUY with TP ₹463
Believe that volume growth + cost efficiencies = strong earnings visibility.

Factors at play
- Firm aluminium prices + higher alumina volumes to boost revenue
- Lower bauxite mining, coal & employee costs to support margins
- New 1 mtpa alumina refinery stream to start commissioning in Q1FY27
- VAP capacity expansion planned over the next 3 years

**Citi on Banks & FCNR-B**

**Key Development**
- RBI FAQ removes ambiguity
- Scope widened significantly
- Overseas branches included
- Scheme becomes more attractive

**Major Change**
- Loans against FCNR(B) allowed
- SBLC issuance permitted
- Lien marking allowed
- Balance-sheet expansion enabled

**Why Positive**
- Beyond funding arbitrage
- Higher deposit mobilization
- Better liquidity profile
- Enhanced capital flexibility

**Additional Benefits**
- CRR exemption
- SLR exemption
- Favorable LCR treatment
- Stronger economics

**Likely Beneficiaries**
- Global banking franchises
- Strong overseas networks
- Faster execution capabilities
- Large private banks

**Key Takeaway**
- Materially positive for banks
- Potentially larger than 2013
- Execution speed critical
- Global reach becomes advantage

**Jefferies on Banks & FCNR-B**

**Key Developments**
- RBI FAQs released
- Foreign branch leveraging allowed
- Tenor-based rates allowed
- Large-ticket pricing flexibility

**Positive Implications**
- Lower execution friction
- Reduced counterparty risk
- Easier deposit mobilisation
- Better product flexibility

**Key Concern**
- Current rates still low
- IRRs may be unattractive
- Mobilisation could remain limited

**What To Watch**
- FCNR-B rate hikes
- 3-year deposit rates
- Large-ticket offerings
- Deposit mobilisation trends

**Key Takeaway**
- RBI changes supportive
- Flexibility improved
- Rate hikes may follow
- Positive for banking sector

**Nomura on Anant Raj**

**Rating & Target**
- Buy maintained
- Target price: ₹650
- TP cut from ₹700

**Estimate Changes**
- FY27 EPS cut: 8%
- FY28 EPS cut: 10%
- Near-term adjustments
- Long-term view intact

**Data Centre Business**
- Revenue visibility improving
- Cloud rentals from Q2 FY27
- New 1.5 MW operational
- DC capacity scaling rapidly

**Growth Triggers**
- Luxury housing launch
- Expected in Q2 FY27
- Real estate monetization
- Data centre ramp-up

**Capacity Outlook**
- FY27 DC: 63 MW
- FY28 DC: 117 MW
- Strong expansion pipeline

**Key Takeaway**
- Data centre story intact
- Cloud revenue to accelerate
- Real estate launch upcoming
- Growth visibility improving

**HSBC on InterGlobe Aviation (IndiGo)**

**Rating & Target**
- Buy maintained
- Target price: ₹5,545

**Key Positives**
- Competitive position strengthening
- Air India facing challenges
- Capacity remains tight
- Industry fares stay firm

**Demand Outlook**
- Near-term momentum strong
- Higher fares supportive
- Fuel surcharge may continue
- Profitability outlook improves

**Sensitivity Analysis**
- 2% yield change:
  - EBITDA impact: ~8%
  - EPS impact: ~49%

- 5% fuel-price change:
  - EBITDA impact: ~10%
  - EPS impact: ~60%

**Key Drivers**
- Domestic fare trends
- Crude oil movement
- Capacity additions
- Competitive intensity

**Key Takeaway**
- IndiGo remains well-positioned
- Strong fare environment
- Lower fuel prices beneficial
- Earnings highly sensitive to yields

**HSBC on Trent**

**Rating & Target**
- Buy maintained
- Target price: ₹3,460
- Valuation: 60x P/E

**AGM Highlights**
- Westside: 50 stores/year
- Zudio: 200-250 stores/year
- Expansion pace unchanged
- Strong growth visibility

**Store Potential**
- Westside potential: 700 stores
- Zudio potential: 5,000 stores
- Higher than estimates
- Long runway remains

**Other Updates**
- Burnt Toast scaling slowly
- Core formats remain focus
- Execution continues strong

**Key Takeaway**
- Massive store opportunity
- Zudio growth intact
- Expansion momentum strong
- Long-term growth visibility high

**Investec on Varun Beverages**

**Rating & Target**
- Buy maintained
- Target price: ₹609

**Key Development**
- Partnership with Asahi
- First non-Pepsi mandate
- Calpis brand launch
- Dairy beverage entry

**Strategic Significance**
- Portfolio diversification
- Expands beverage offerings
- New growth avenue
- Reduces Pepsi dependence

**Future Optionality**
- Alcobev clause added
- SPV restriction removed
- Broader Asahi partnership possible
- Large addressable opportunity

**Potential Opportunities**
- Asahi global brands
- Premium beverage portfolio
- India market expansion
- International market access

**Key Takeaway**
- Strategic partnership milestone
- Diversification story strengthens
- Significant optionality ahead
- Potential long-term rerating trigger

**Nomura on Tata Motors PV**

**Rating & Target**
- Neutral maintained
- Target price: ₹373

**FY31 Ambitions**
- Market share: 20%
- Current share: 14%
- PV market to double
- Strong volume growth target

**Growth Drivers**
- 6 new models
- 20+ refreshes planned
- 4 new EV launches
- EV and CNG focus

**Powertrain Mix**
- EV mix: 30%+
- CNG mix: 25%+
- Multi-powertrain strategy
- Portfolio diversification

**Margin Outlook**
- FY29 EBITDA: 8%
- FY31 EBITDA: 10%
- EV business profitable
- ICE costs down 5-6%

**JLR Outlook**
- Commodity cost pressure
- FY27 margins impacted
- Long-term targets intact

**Key Takeaway**
- Ambitious FY31 roadmap
- EVs and CNG key drivers
- Margin expansion expected
- Execution remains crucial

**Citi on Tata Motors PV**

**Rating & Target**
- Sell maintained
- Target price: ₹320

**India PV Outlook**
- FY29 revenue: ₹1.15 lakh Cr
- FY31 revenue: ₹1.4 lakh Cr
- Strong growth guidance
- Revenue drivers under watch

**Margin Guidance**
- FY29 EBITDA: 8%
- FY31 EBITDA: 10%
- Broadly in line
- Profitability improving

**Group Outlook**
- FY29 revenue: ₹5 lakh Cr
- FY31 revenue: ₹6 lakh Cr
- EBIT target: 7%-10%
- Healthy growth expected

**JLR Outlook**
- Revenue CAGR: ~12%
- FY29 EBIT: ~8%
- FY31 EBIT: ~12%
- Margin expansion expected

**Key Takeaway**
- Strong revenue targets
- Margins improving steadily
- Citi remains cautious
- Execution remains key

**CLSA on Tata Motors PV**

**Rating & Target**
- Outperform maintained
- Target price: ₹452

**Growth Outlook**
- PV industry growth: 10% FY27
- Market share target: 20%
- FY31 target timeline
- Volume CAGR: 15%

**Growth Drivers**
- 6 new nameplates
- 20+ refreshes planned
- EV portfolio expansion
- CNG segment focus

**PV Margin Targets**
- FY26 EBITDA: 5%
- FY29 EBITDA: 8%
- FY31 EBITDA: 10%
- Localization driving gains

**Consolidated Outlook**
- Revenue CAGR: 12%
- FY29 EBIT: 7%
- FY31 EBIT: 10%
- Profitability improving

**JLR Outlook**
- Revenue CAGR: 10%
- FY29 EBIT: ~8%
- Premium demand support
- Margin improvement expected

**Key Takeaway**
- Strong FY31 roadmap
- EV and CNG key levers
- Margin expansion visible
- Market share gains targeted

**Nomura on Tata Motors CV**

**Rating & Target**
- Neutral maintained
- Target price: ₹402

**FY28 Targets**
- Market share: 40%
- FY26 share: 35.7%
- Focus on share gains
- Margin discipline continues

**Profitability Goals**
- Double-digit EBITDA margins
- Teen margins in upcycles
- ROCE: 30-35%
- FCF margin: 7-9%

**Growth Strategy**
- Non-cyclical growth focus
- Spares and services
- Exports expansion
- Digital business scaling

**Technology & Scale**
- Connected vehicles: 30 lakh
- Current base: 10 lakh
- Platform ecosystem expansion

**Iveco Acquisition**
- Completion by Q2 FY27
- Sourcing synergies
- Product-sharing benefits
- LATAM expansion opportunity

**Key Takeaway**
- Strong long-term targets
- Margin focus intact
- Iveco key growth catalyst
- Non-cyclical mix increasing

**HSBC on Tata Motors CV**

**Rating & Target**
- Buy maintained
- Target price: ₹490

**Key Views**
- Near-term demand strong
- Fuel-price impact temporary
- 2H demand trend crucial
- Industry outlook remains healthy

**Business Outlook**
- Strong M&HCV position
- Market leadership intact
- LCV weakness bottoming out
- Recovery signs emerging

**Key Triggers**
- M&HCV demand strength
- LCV volume recovery
- Freight activity trends
- Market-share stability

**Key Takeaway**
- Commercial vehicle cycle healthy
- M&HCV remains key strength
- LCV recovery can aid growth
- 2H demand is critical monitor

**CLSA on Tata Motors CV**

**Rating & Target**
- Outperform maintained
- Target price: ₹527

**Growth Outlook**
- High single-digit industry growth
- Market share target: 40%
- Current share: 36%
- New launches to support growth

**Demand Drivers**
- Freight growth linked to GDP
- Additional 10 lakh CV demand
- Parked fleet: 40-45 lakh units
- Replacement cycle supportive

**Strategic Initiatives**
- Iveco acquisition by Q2 FY27
- Focus on non-cyclical businesses
- Diversification strategy ongoing
- Growth above core CV business

**Financial Targets**
- Double-digit EBITDA margins
- Mid-teen margins in upcycle
- ROCE: 30-35%
- FCF: 7-9% of revenue

**Key Takeaway**
- Strong CV growth outlook
- Market share gains targeted
- Healthy profitability guidance
- Iveco acquisition key catalyst

**CLSA on Vedanta Aluminium**

**Rating & Target**
- Outperform initiated
- Target price: ₹540

**Investment Thesis**
- Strong aluminium cycle
- Operational tailwinds strong
- Volume growth visibility
- Cost profile improving

**Key Drivers**
- Backward integration benefits
- Lower production costs
- Global cost competitiveness
- Margin expansion potential

**Financial Outlook**
- Strong free cash flow
- Deleveraging potential
- Dividend payout support
- Healthy cash generation

**Valuation View**
- Trading at discount
- Below domestic peers
- Below global peers
- Re-rating potential seen

**Key Takeaway**
- Cost leadership improving
- Strong FCF generation
- Beneficiary of aluminium cycle
- Valuation gap may narrow

Target Price ₹367

Recommendation Equal-weight

Investor Day focus was on sustained volume growth, premiumization

Closer integration of JLR and India businesses to drive sustainable and profitable growth

Industry dynamics remain supportive, with premiumization a key structural trend

CITI ON TATA MOTORS CV

Mgmt. guided to high-single-digit CV industry volume growth in FY27

Targets 40% domestic CV market share (VAHAN-based) vs 35.7% in FY26

Double-digit EBITDA margin and 30-35% ROCE post IVECO acquisition by FY28

Company plans to maintain investment spend at 2-4% of revenue

Core product strategy is centered on addressing white spaces in trucks and bus

CLSA On Adani Ent

INITIATE OVERWEIGHT

TARGET PRICE: ₹3,638

India's Premier Incubator Across Airports, Roads, Data Centers, New Energy, Copper, PVC, Mining & Defence

EBITDA Expected To Grow Around 3x By FY30

Growth Led By Airports, New Energy & Primary Industries

FY27 Catalysts: NMIA, Ganga Expressway, Copper Plant, New Energy Expansion

FY30: 145 m Airport Passengers & 2 GW Data Center Portfolio (JV Route)

**Morgan Stanley on Adani Enterprises**

**Rating & Target**
- Overweight initiated
- Target price: ₹3,638

**Investment Thesis**
- India's premier incubator
- Exposure to mega themes
- Infrastructure-led growth
- Diverse business portfolio

**Key Growth Areas**
- Airports
- Roads
- Data centres
- New energy
- Copper
- Mining
- Defence

**FY27 Triggers**
- NMIA commissioning
- Ganga Expressway progress
- Copper plant ramp-up
- New energy expansion

**FY30 Outlook**
- EBITDA to grow 3x
- Airports: 145 mn passengers
- Data centre: 2 GW portfolio
- Multiple businesses scaling

**Key Takeaway**
- Strong incubation platform
- Multiple growth engines
- Large infrastructure exposure
- Long-term EBITDA expansion

CITI ON BEL

Target Price ₹515

Recommendation Accumulate

Recent Developments Incrementally Positive

Reiterate a Top Pick

Recent order wins are spread across multiple product categories

Timing of large-value QRSAM conversion remains a monitorable

Think the combination of steady base orders, improving macro and incremental export optionality

This should provide continued conviction in BEL's medium-term growth visibility

India is set to launch 50 to 60 new mines this fiscal year, building on the 36 operationalized in the previous year. The government is also fast-tracking bids for lithium and nickel processing units within three months. Significant progress is being made in critical mineral extraction and exploration projects, with targets expected to be met ahead of schedule. The private sector is also actively participating in exploration

https://m.economictimes.com/industry/indl-goods/svs/metals-mining/india-plans-to-operationalise-up-to-60-mines-in-fy27/articleshow/131949489.cms

MACQUARIE ON SYNGENE

Target Price ₹735

Earlier Target ₹835

Recommendation Outperform

From Transition to Transformation

Syngene's stock price correction factors in headwinds for its CMO business from 1 product

But fails to capture upside from medium-term growth levers

New mgmt is prioritising stronger commercial execution

FY27 guidance appears conservative

CITI ON TATA MOTORS PV

Target Price ₹320

Recommendation Sell

Positively surprised by the very strong revenue CAGR for India business

It will be interesting to see how much of it is driven by volumes/pricing/ASP

EBITDA margin guidance of 8%/10% is in line with estimates

India business growth guidance is very strong

Domestic Industry outlook is healthy

MORGAN STANLEY ON

ADANI ENTERPRISES

Target Price ₹3638

Recommendation Overweight

Anchored to "The New India"

India's premier incubator, with exposure to multi-decade themes

Forecast EBITDA to scale up 3x by FY30, driven by airports, new energy, and primary industries

FY27 marks an earnings inflection for Adani Enterprises

FY27 drivers: NMIA, Ganga Expressway, copper plant, & new energy capacity expansion

CITI ON POWER

Recent Power Demand Pick-Up: Key Signals for H2

Power demand metrics have recovered robustly in Q1

Near-term demand trends will likely moderate on monsoon onset

Capex upcycle is already ongoing, see likelihood of policy measures in H2

Remain constructive on electric utility stocks

NTPC is top pick supported by relatively undemanding valuations

CITI ON BANKS

Clarification reframes the scheme's character entirely

From cost-arbitrage instrument into a full-spectrum balance sheet expansion play

Layered atop CRR/SLR exemption & favorable LCR treatment

It can potentially attracts higher foreign deposits/borrowings flow than 2013

Scheme's ultimate efficacy will be determined by execution velocity

Alpha accrues decisively to institutions that move fastest and leverage deepest

NOMURA ON ANANT RAJ

Target Price ₹650

Earlier Target ₹700

Recommendation Accumulate

Visibility on revenues for cloud segment should improve from Q2

Luxury Group Housing Project likely to be launched in Q2

FY27F/28F EPS cut by 8%/10% on slower-than-expected ramp-up of cloud capacity

CLSA ON PRICE ACTION

Midcap breakout building, large caps range-bound

Nifty Midcap 100 is pressing against a cup-and-handle breakout (62,113-62,907)

Flag AB Capital and Nykaa as buy candidates

Turning to the large-cap Nifty index, there is limited change

In contrast, the Nifty remains rangebound (21,743-26,319)

UBS ON INFO EDGE

Target Price ₹1280

Recommendation Accumulate

Incremental upside with rising Al and deeptech relevance

Al portfolio scaling with early entry and improving validation

Deeptech in early stages and consumer tech remains anchor
